question: Q-p1-closed-loop-causal-weight-learning
conjecture: On PhantomWiki universes, an agent whose retrievals leave episode-ID-indexed eligibility tags, and whose edge slow-weights receive candidate commits Delta_ell = eta * (r_e - r_hat_e) * tag_strength gated by the existing absorption FSM (freeze -> fresh-eval -> canary -> CAS), shows improving sealed retrieval behavior over episodes on fresh unseen questions, while (A2) no-commit, (A3) timestamp-shuffled M, and (A4) uniform untagged commit controls do not. This is the minimal cell of the canon claim: outcome -> judgment -> DeltaW -> next behavior differs, with the environment (sealed answers) as critic to avoid the free-label trap that voided llm_judgment_loop.py.

learning_rule.tag: every hyperedge in a winning trace of episode e gets tag_strength = its normalized contribution in the best trace; tags are indexed by episode ID, no wall-clock decay (verdict delay regime, tribunal sec.3)
learning_rule.reward: r_e = set-match answer accuracy of episode e against sealed PhantomWiki gold (post-hoc, never visible during walk)
learning_rule.baseline: r_hat_e = expanding mean of r_1..r_{e-1} within arm A1; episode 1 has M = 0 (no commit)
learning_rule.commit: candidate Delta_ell(b) = clip(eta * M_e * tag_strength(b), keeps ell <= 0 open-kernel contract; positive M moves toward 0, negative M moves down) through hswm_absorption_fsm: FREEZE -> EVALUATE on fresh questions -> CANARY regression probe (previously-correct questions must not break beyond epsilon_c) -> CAS ACTIVATE or REJECT/ROLLBACK
learning_rule.plane_scope: weight-plane (slow ell) only. Topology, router, embeddings frozen. Single agent.

arms.A2_no_commit: identical walk, weights frozen after episode 1 (ablation: does removing the loop remove the behavior change)
arms.A3_shuffled_M: identical tags and update budget, but the M sequence is permuted across episodes (same multiset; tribunal-imported timestamp-shuffle falsifier for M causality)
arms.A4_uniform_commit: identical total update L1 budget per episode, spread uniformly over all edges touched that episode regardless of tag strength (controls that credit assignment, not update magnitude, drives any gain)

prediction.secondary: linear slope of A1 sealed recall@10 across episodes > 0
prediction.novel_metric: tag_utility_spearman: correlation between sign of committed Delta_ell on an edge and that edge's re-use in winning traces in later episodes
prediction.credence_note: first ever closed loop on this substrate; the only prior judgment-loop measurement was -0.012 (harmful). credence is deliberately below 0.5.

abandon_signal_disclosure.justification: This prereg IS the cure the signal demands: the next judged node on this branch will carry a preregistered prediction and a measured verdict, converting a theory-only branch into a judged one. No further theory nodes will be added before this measurement.
abandon_signal_disclosure.commitment: If A1 fails K1 below, the three-factor weight-learning line on this substrate is abandoned and the programme falls back to the ExpeL-style text-lesson baseline, per canon K1. Recorded before measurement.

already_public_before_registration.note: Only structural facts were observed on PhantomWiki universes (article/fact/question counts, hop distributions from R3 groundwork). No arm outcome, recall, or delta for THIS experiment has been computed or inspected. Learning-loop modules do not exist yet; no measurement is possible before implementation freeze.
already_public_before_registration.prior_context: R3 groundwork 2026-07-23 (deterministic ingest, density dial, sealed answers); tribunal 2026-07-23 (dual-timescale eligibility, shuffle falsifier, stability budget imports); plasticity PROM 2026-07-23 (eligibility/commit contract sec.7, FSM reuse sec.9).

kill_conditions: K1: mean paired recall@10 gain (A1 - A2) <= 0.01 or bootstrap95 lower <= 0 -> three-factor weight learning on this substrate fails; fall back to text-lesson baseline per canon K1.
K2: A3_shuffled_M >= A1 -> the M signal is not causal; stop learning runs and invest in verdict quality before any retry (canon K2 analog).
K3: A4_uniform_commit >= A1 -> tag-based credit assignment is worthless on this substrate; the eligibility mechanism (not the loop) is rejected.
K4: canary regressions exceed epsilon_c in any episode -> halt, fix homeostasis/gate first; no further commits until canary holds.
K5: tag_utility_spearman <= 0 -> committed updates do not track subsequent utility; credit assignment signal-to-noise insufficient (do not scale up).
void_conditions: any gold consumption during graph construction or walk
any LLM/network call during graph construction (deterministic ingest only)
inspecting any arm outcome before module sha freeze is appended to this file
any wall-clock decay on tags (regime violation: verdicts arrive at episode granularity)

scope_boundary.corpus: synthetic PhantomWiki only; real-prose transfer is not claimed
scope_boundary.headline_metric: retrieval-side best-trace recall@10 on fresh questions; answer accuracy is the reward input, not the headline claim
scope_boundary.claim: a causal closed loop exists (outcome -> DeltaW -> changed next-episode behavior). NOT claimed: answer-quality uplift, multi-agent transfer (P2), topology learning (P3), sleep/consolidation (P4)
scope_boundary.llm_role: the walker/reasoner function only (where used); the critic in P1 is the environment's sealed answers, deliberately not an LLM judge, to avoid the free-label/self-judgment trap

locked_parameters.walker: strict T3 walker (mu-additive, max-product K<=2), identical across arms
locked_parameters.universe: large sparse PhantomWiki (t200, friendship-k 1, question-depth 10), same generation flags as R3

preflight_amendment.reason: The frozen R1 preflight found 390 eligible non-aggregation questions with bounded trace gold, while 200 episode plus 200 fresh-gate questions required 400. No arm retrieval, answer, reward, recall, delta, or verdict was computed before refusal.
preflight_amendment.change: fresh_questions_per_episode only: 40 -> 38, yielding 200 episode plus 190 disjoint fresh-gate questions. Episode count, questions per episode, learning rule, arms, thresholds, bootstrap, model, and all outcome criteria remain unchanged.
